title: Disposing of medication — returning unused stock to the pharmacy
source: CM08 Safe Disposal of Medication Policy and Procedure
scopeNote: Standard pharmacy return only. Controlled drug disposal is a separate process (Controlled Drugs Policy) and is not shown in this animation.

steps.label: Get the person's consent
steps.caption: The medication belongs to the person, so ask before you return it.

steps.label: Write down what you're returning
steps.caption: Record the date, the person's name, the medicine, and the quantity.

steps.label: Remove or cross out the label
steps.caption: Take off the pharmacy label and shred it, or black out the details.
steps.visualNote: Show the label being obscured to protect personal information.

steps.label: Put it in the pharmacy box
steps.caption: Use the disposal container the pharmacy gave you — never the bin or toilet.
steps.visualNote: Contrast the labelled pharmacy container with a crossed-out bin and toilet.

steps.label: Lock it away, separate from in-use meds
steps.caption: Keep the container locked and apart from medication you are still giving.

steps.label: Return to pharmacy and sign
steps.caption: The pharmacy collects it, and you both sign to confirm the handover.

endFrame: Never bin it, never flush it — always back to the pharmacy with a record.
